approach: one spec at a time; the retired golden-set is reference only, nothing is migrated or copied
the CFG is the source of truth — update the spec, draw the control-flow graph, then re-derive the whole scenario set from the graph edge by edge; do not read the suite and patch only the gaps
a legacy case is a claim to verify against the current implementation, never evidence of current behavior
additive scenarios only on the frozen suite; any narrowing would be Clearance-bound and stop the mission
self-assert the spec gate within leash, bring the impl gate to the owner
blast: low — one node's suite plus its README; no engine, no shared instrument, no other suite touched; the implementation was not changed
reversibility: high — two additive scenarios on an unmerged branch, revertable without touching any pre-existing contract
novelty: low — the node, its suite and its implementation all already exist; this widens a contract to bind a second arm of two classifier forks rather than introducing a capability
confidence: high — cold spec-judge returned ALIGNED (both additions are consistency-completions that kill the surviving classifier mutants, not over-speccing) and a focused cold impl-judge confirmed both pass the unchanged SKILL

why: Re-drove the suite from the skillify SKILL.md control-flow graph (ROUTE activation fork with four sibling deferrals -> MINE the session -> resolve PLACEMENT -> resolve PATTERN -> DRAFT -> WRITE -> VALIDATE -> place+link, under the generalize-not-transcribe and never-hand-off-unvalidated guards). The 17 golden cases mapped near-1:1 to the 18 frozen scenarios, but drawing the two 3-way classifier forks surfaced that each bound only ONE arm: PLACEMENT was bound to project-private and PATTERN to process, both folded into a single Given ('an ordered multi-step process scoped to contributors -> project-private placement and process pattern'). A second distinct arm per fork is required to kill the arm-collapse mutant class — one bound arm kills every constant mapping except always-[that-arm]. Added two additive scenarios binding a second arm each ('a personal session workflow resolves to the user placement'; 'a tool-centered session workflow resolves to the tool-based pattern'), structurally additive by gherkin-cli (added two, zero modified, zero removed) so the freeze is preserved. A cold spec-judge re-derived each oracle: confirmed both map verbatim onto real SKILL table rows (personal/no-codebase -> user; tool/external-system-centered -> tool-based), confirmed they kill M1 (always-process) and the surviving M2 arm-collapse (collapse placement arms to project-private while still asking when ambiguous), confirmed both are single-axis / not double-barreled / disjoint-Given specializations that cannot contradict the frozen folded scenario, and confirmed the two-arm floor is the correct discrimination floor for the constant-mapping class. Verdict ALIGNED. The node spec was rebuilt to the four-section shape (What / Use Cases / Control Flow with a mermaid CFG / Scenario map) with every edge bound one-to-one; a scenario-map lint failure was fixed in-pass (the @trigger annotation had to move out of the backtick-wrapped scenario cell so check-suite matches the title). The two-arm floor leaves the third arms (standard pattern, project-public placement) as inputs only, never derived outputs, so a single-arm-swap mutant on those arms survives — a deliberate, documented scoping choice recorded here rather than closed, since binding a third arm adds no constant-mapping kill. Root project status and its human ratification left untouched: additive scenarios self-clear.

note: The frozen scenario 'placement and pattern are resolved from the session signal' folds two orthogonal classifier axes into one boolean Then (project-private placement AND process pattern). Legal (boolean @behavior conjunction, not a @rubric dimension) but low diagnostic locality — a failure does not say which axis broke. Clearance-bound to split into a placement->project-private scenario and a pattern->process scenario. Splitting alone would NOT have killed the classifier mutants; the two second-arm additions this mission were the load-bearing fix. Deferred: splitting a frozen scenario needs Clearance and is out of scope for an additive pass.

SKILL-level (impl, not suite): the skillify pattern table gives no tiebreak when a session workflow is BOTH ordered-multi-step AND tool-centered — both are listed as pattern signals (Process vs Tool-based) with no precedence. The suite avoids the collision by keeping the two Givens on disjoint snapshots, but the underlying SKILL ambiguity is unresolved; a future mission should state the precedence before a scenario can bind the overlap. Likewise the placement table's overlap (a personal workflow that is also repo-scoped) is untiebroken.
